title: lindernia dubia subsp. major
intro: Lindernia dubia subsp. major, also known as false lipenia or false water-pimpernel, is a plant species belonging to the Linderniaceae family, characterized by its tiny white flowers and submerged or emergent growth habit. Traditionally, Lindernia dubia subsp. major has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lindernia dubia subsp. major are believed to include the reduction of inflammation, improved wound healing, and the treatment of various infections, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Lindernia dubia subsp. major can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, typically made by steeping the plant's leaves and stems in hot water, although specific herbal preparations and dosages should be determined under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
